task:
Ticket 0090, Atom 1 of N — guard `oz resume` against forking a DUPLICATE orchestrator under the same run id (the destructive defect #1). This atom fixes ONLY the refuse-to-fork guard; do not attempt the larger 'continue parked run in place / re-attach' work (that is a later atom).

BACKGROUND (verify before changing — do not patch from this summary):
- `cocoder oz resume <runId>` -> `resumeRun(ctx, runId)` in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts (~line 1248). It requires run.status==='held' and checks ctx.inFlight, then calls `launchRun(... { resumeHeldRunId: runId })`.
- The runner core `runRun` in packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts UNCONDITIONALLY calls `sessionHost.spawn(...)` for Oscar (~line 681) and Bob (~line 707) even on the resume path (existingRun !== null). So resume ALWAYS spawns a FRESH Oscar/Bob/Deb session set; it never re-attaches. On a run whose panes are still live this produces TWO orchestrators on one run id (run_294 incident).
- A live-session primitive already exists and is the one to reuse (do NOT invent a parallel one): `ctx.store.listSessions(runId)` filtered by `ctx.liveRefs.has(session.sessionRef)`, with `session.persona` ('oscar'|'bob'|'deb'). See the exact pattern at launcher.ts ~line 1384 (`liveOscar`) and ~2739.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
Add a guard so a resume that would spawn a fresh session set for a run that STILL HAS a live tracked agent session refuses to fork. Return an HTTP-shaped refusal (status 409) with a stable `code` (e.g. 'run-has-live-sessions') and a clear, actionable message: explain that the run already has live panes, that forking a second orchestrator under the same run id is unsafe, and point to the correct continuation path — deliver the founder's answer through Oz's founder-answer channel (which re-prompts the existing pane), or tear the run down first. Do NOT spawn anything when refusing.

Place the guard so it protects the operator/CLI `oz resume` footgun. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT — you MUST NOT break the sanctioned founder-answer resume path: `requestFounderAnswer` (launcher.ts ~1275) writes founder-answer.json then calls `resumeRun`. Investigate whether, in the sanctioned flow, the parked run's panes are still live at resume time; design the guard so the sanctioned founder-answer resume still succeeds (e.g. by gating only the manual/CLI entrypoint, or by a parameter that the sanctioned path passes, whichever is the honest, minimal-surface fix — decide based on the actual code, and explain your choice). If you discover the sanctioned path ALSO orphans/duplicates live panes, do NOT fix that here — note it precisely in your closeout as the seam for the next atom, and keep this atom's guard correct for the CLI footgun.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. A manual `oz resume <runId>` on a held run that has a live tracked Oscar (or other agent) session refuses with status 409, a stable code, and an actionable message — and spawns NO new session (assert sessionHost.spawn is not called).
2. The sanctioned `requestFounderAnswer` -> resume path is proven to still work via a test (resume proceeds when it should).
3. New/updated unit tests cover both: reuse and extend packages/core/tests/runner-founder-stop-resume.test.ts and/or the daemon launcher tests. No existing test is weakened or deleted to pass.
4. Reuse the existing live-session detection primitive (store.listSessions ∩ liveRefs); do not add a second parallel session-liveness contract.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: teardown, normal launch, the founder-answer resume, and the held-run status machine. Run the repo test Play (the deterministic test command) and report the actual command, exit code, and the relevant test names passing. Do not run git; the runner commits on verify pass.

Ticket 0090, Atom 1 (RE-SCOPED after rejection) — implement ONLY the refuse-to-fork guard in the daemon launcher. The prior attempt was rejected for exceeding scope (it also added runner.ts re-attach and a throw-on-dead-pane that broke recovery). This atom is the launcher guard and its tests, NOTHING else.

START CLEAN: discard/do not carry forward any runner.ts, prompts.ts, or cli changes from the prior attempt. If the working tree still contains them, revert those files to HEAD first. The ONLY production file this atom may modify is packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts.

WHAT TO DO (this is exactly the guard the verifier already judged correct — reproduce just this):
In `resumeRun` (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts ~line 1248), after the `run.status !== 'held'` check and BEFORE the `ctx.inFlight` check, add a refuse-to-fork guard:
- Compute live tracked agent sessions for this run: `ctx.store.listSessions(runId).filter((session) => ctx.liveRefs.has(session.sessionRef))`. Put this in a small private helper `liveTrackedAgentSessions(ctx, runId)` returning `{persona, sessionRef}[]`.
- Treat a founder-answer-driven resume as sanctioned and EXEMPT from the guard: `const isFounderAnswerResume = opts.founderAnswer !== undefined && opts.founderAnswer !== null`.
- If `!isFounderAnswerResume && liveSessions.length > 0`, return `{ status: 409, body: { error: <clear actionable message naming the live personas; say forking a second orchestrator under the same run id is unsafe; tell the operator to deliver the founder's answer through Oz's founder-answer channel which re-prompts the existing pane, or tear the run down before using resume>, code: 'run-has-live-sessions', runId, liveSessions: liveSessions.map(s => ({persona: s.persona, sessionRef: s.sessionRef})) } }` and spawn NOTHING.

DO NOT, in this atom: modify runner.ts, prompts.ts, packages/cli/**, or any spawn/reattach logic; change holdRun or park-time pane behavior; add any throw-on-dead-pane logic. The 'continue parked run in place / re-attach' work and the park-pane policy are a SEPARATE later atom awaiting a founder design decision — explicitly leave them out and do not touch the founder-answer prompt wording.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (tests live in the daemon package, e.g. packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts — extend, do not weaken existing tests):
1. A manual `resumeRun(ctx, runId)` (NO founderAnswer) on a held run that has a live tracked Oscar session returns status 409 with code 'run-has-live-sessions', lists the live persona(s), and triggers NO sessionHost.spawn.
2. `resumeRun(ctx, runId, { founderAnswer: '<text>' })` on the same held-with-live-Oscar state is NOT blocked by this guard (it proceeds past the guard — assert it does not return the 'run-has-live-sessions' 409).
3. A held run with NO live tracked sessions is NOT blocked by this guard.
4. The helper reuses the existing `store.listSessions ∩ ctx.liveRefs` primitive; no second session-liveness contract is introduced.

Run the repo test command (`pnpm -r test`, or at minimum `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` plus `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`) and report the actual command, exit code, and the relevant passing test names. Do not run git; the runner commits on verify pass.

Ticket 0090, Atom 2 — implement 'continue a parked run in place' via per-persona pane RE-ATTACH on resume, with a fresh-respawn fallback when a pane is dead. Founder approved park-pane policy: KEEP panes alive on park (do NOT change holdRun), and on resume re-attach to the existing live panes instead of spawning a duplicate set. THE REGRESSION THAT GOT THE FIRST ATTEMPT REJECTED MUST NOT RECUR: a stored pane that is not live must FALL BACK to a fresh spawn for that persona — never throw, never leave a held run unresumable.

FILES: packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts is the primary change (the Oscar/Bob/Deb spawn block ~lines 626-772). You MAY add a small private helper in runner.ts. Do NOT modify the atom-1 launcher guard (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts resumeRun) — it stays as committed. Do NOT touch holdRun or park-time pane teardown. Minimal prompt-wording edits in prompts.ts are allowed ONLY if needed to keep the founder-answer dispatch accurate; prefer leaving prompts.ts alone.

BEHAVIOR (resume path = existingRun !== null; the runner already reads resume-state and weaves the founder answer into the Oscar launch prompt via resumeFounderDecision — keep that):
Per persona (Oscar, Bob, and Deb when present), at spawn time on the resume path:
  - Look up the latest stored session for that persona via store.listSessions(run.id).
  - If that stored session's pane is LIVE (probe with sessionHost.status(ref).state === 'running', guarded by try/catch treating any error as not-live), RE-ATTACH: reconstruct the SessionRef from the stored session, build the driver from it (createPaneOscarDriver / createPaneBuilderDriver; for Deb reuse the existing live ref), do NOT call sessionHost.spawn for that persona, and record a 'session-reused' event {persona, ref}. For Oscar, after reattaching, send the orchestrator launch prompt to the existing pane (oscarDriver.send(oscarLaunchPrompt)) so the founder answer / continuation reaches the live pane. For Bob, reattach to standby (no prompt). 
  - If the stored session is ABSENT or NOT live, FALL BACK to the normal fresh spawn for that persona exactly as a non-resume launch does (carrying the same prompts/answer). Never throw on a dead/absent pane.
Headless mode is unaffected — keep the existing headless branches; reattach applies only to pane (non-headless) mode.
The non-resume (fresh launch, existingRun === null) path must be byte-for-byte unchanged in behavior.

WHY THE FALLBACK MATTERS: a held run can sit parked while its panes die (founder closes them, host restart). Resume must still recover by respawning fresh. The first attempt used a requireLiveResumeSession that threw — that made dead-pane held runs unresumable and was rejected. Do not reintroduce any throw-on-dead-pane.

INTERACTION TO PRESERVE (verify, don't just trust): with this change, (a) founder-answer resume [resumeRun with founderAnswer] is exempt from the atom-1 guard, enters runRun, and RE-ATTACHES to the live Oscar pane delivering the answer — no duplicate; (b) manual resume of a held run whose panes are DEAD passes the atom-1 guard (no live sessions) and RESPAWNS fresh via the fallback; (c) manual resume with LIVE panes is still refused by the atom-1 guard before ever reaching runRun.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (tests in packages/core — extend runner-founder-stop-resume.test.ts and/or runner test files; do NOT weaken or delete existing tests):
1. Resume with a LIVE stored Oscar pane re-attaches: NO new Oscar sessionHost.spawn, a 'session-reused' event for oscar is recorded, and oscarDriver.send is called with the launch prompt (carrying the founder answer where applicable).
2. Resume with a DEAD/absent stored Oscar pane FALLS BACK to a fresh spawn (sessionHost.spawn IS called for oscar) and the run resumes normally — NO throw. THIS IS THE REGRESSION GUARD; it must exist and pass.
3. Bob and Deb follow the same per-persona reattach-if-live / respawn-if-dead rule (cover at least Bob; Deb if the harness makes it practical).
4. Fresh (non-resume) launch behavior and the existing resume tests are unchanged and still green.
5. No second session-liveness contract: reuse store.listSessions + sessionHost.status.

Run the repo test command (at minimum `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` since the guard interaction matters) and a typecheck (`tsc --noEmit` on the touched packages). Report the actual command, exit code, and the relevant passing test names — including the dead-pane fallback test by name. Do not run git; the runner commits on verify pass.

Ticket 0090, Atom 3 (final) — harden the founder-continue dispatch wording so a parked orchestrator is steered to the founder-answer lane and explicitly away from `oz resume`. This closes the model-gap the ticket self-diagnosed (Oscar reached for `cocoder oz resume` because the dispatch called the mechanism 'the Oz resume path') and corrects wording that atom 2 made stale.

FILE: packages/core/src/runner/prompts.ts — function `buildFounderContinueDispatch` (~line 677). Update ONLY this function's returned string (and its test). Do not touch runner.ts, launcher.ts, or any other behavior.

CURRENT TEXT (to replace):
`The runner is parking this run now. Do not write ${nextDirectivePath} in this pane after the founder answers; no live runner will be waiting for it. The founder must answer through the Oz resume path, for example: founder-answer ${runId} <answer>. That records the answer, resumes this held run, and the resumed runner will prompt you to write the continuation directive at ${nextDirectivePath}.`

REQUIRED CHANGES in the new wording:
1. STOP calling it 'the Oz resume path'. Name it the founder-answer lane and give BOTH concrete forms: Oz chat `founder-answer ${runId} <answer>` and CLI `cocoder oz founder-answer ${runId} <answer>`.
2. Add an explicit negative: do NOT run `cocoder oz resume ${runId}` to continue after a founder decision — that is the wrong tool and (per the new guard) is refused while panes are live.
3. Correct the now-stale claim. Atom 2 made resume RE-ATTACH to the live panes in place. So replace 'Do not write ${nextDirectivePath} in this pane after the founder answers; no live runner will be waiting for it' with accurate guidance: do not proactively write ${nextDirectivePath} yourself now; once the founder answers through the founder-answer lane, the run resumes and the runner will re-prompt you (in these same live panes when they are still attached) to write the continuation directive at ${nextDirectivePath}.
4. Keep the leading `FOUNDER DECISION NEEDED — surface this question to the founder without wrapping the run:\n\n${question}\n\n` preamble and the parking statement intact.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. The updated `buildFounderContinueDispatch` string contains: the `founder-answer ${runId}` form, the `cocoder oz founder-answer ${runId}` form, an explicit do-NOT-run-`oz resume` instruction, and the corrected in-place-reattach guidance. It no longer contains the phrase 'the Oz resume path' and no longer claims no live runner will be waiting.
2. Update the corresponding assertion(s) in packages/core/tests/prompts.test.ts to pin the new wording (the do-not-`oz resume` instruction and the founder-answer lane), without weakening unrelated assertions. If any other test asserts the old 'Oz resume path'/'no live runner' phrasing, update it to the new contract.
3. `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` is green and `tsc --noEmit` on core exits 0. Report the command, exit code, and the prompts test name(s) passing.

Do not run git; the runner commits on verify pass.

Ticket 0090, Atom 4 — reconcile the resume guard with the atom-2 reattach: the atom-1 refuse-to-fork guard in resumeRun now BREAKS the documented founder-halt resume-in-place flow and is redundant with (and weaker than) the per-persona reattach. Remove it and let reattach be the single owner of resume-pane handling.

WHY (verify against the code before changing): atom 2 made runRun RE-ATTACH to live Oscar/Bob/Deb panes on resume (and respawn fresh on dead panes). So `cocoder oz resume <runId>` on a held run with live panes should CONTINUE IN PLACE, never fork. But atom 1 added a guard in resumeRun (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts ~line 1251) that returns 409 code 'run-has-live-sessions' for any non-founder-answer resume when live tracked sessions exist. A FOUNDER-HALT hold (ADR-0037) parks `held` with panes OPEN and is resumed via `cocoder oz resume <runId>` (no founder answer) — so that guard now REFUSES the legitimate founder-halt resume-in-place, with a message that nonsensically tells the founder to use the founder-answer channel (a halt has no question). The guard is also weaker than reattach: it keys on the in-memory ctx.liveRefs set, which is EMPTY after a daemon restart (fails open exactly when a real fork could happen), whereas reattach uses the durable stored sessionRef + sessionHost.status probe.

WHAT TO CHANGE (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts):
1. In resumeRun, DELETE the refuse-to-fork guard block added in atom 1: the `isFounderAnswerResume` / `liveSessions` computation and the `if (!isFounderAnswerResume && liveSessions.length > 0) { return 409 'run-has-live-sessions' ... }` early return. resumeRun should proceed to launchRun for any held run; reattach (in runRun) handles live panes (reattach) vs dead panes (respawn).
2. DELETE the now-unused private helper `liveTrackedAgentSessions(ctx, runId)`.
3. KEEP the existing `run.status !== 'held'` guard and the `ctx.inFlight.has(workspaceId)` guard unchanged — those are correct and unrelated.

TESTS (packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts):
4. The atom-1 test 'resumeRun refuses manual resume when the held run still has a live tracked agent session' is now asserting the wrong behavior — REPLACE it with a test proving manual `resumeRun(ctx, runId)` (NO founderAnswer) on a held run with a live tracked Oscar session is NOT refused with 'run-has-live-sessions' and proceeds (status 202 resuming) — i.e. it no longer pre-empts reattach.
5. KEEP the 'founder-answer path is not blocked' test (still valid; it now proves the same proceed behavior via the answer path).

CORE COVERAGE (packages/core/tests/runner-founder-stop-resume.test.ts):
6. Add a test that resume with NO founder answer (founder-halt style: resumeRunId set, resumeFounderAnswer absent) and a LIVE stored Oscar pane RE-ATTACHES (no Oscar spawn, session-reused recorded) — proving founder-halt resume continues in place. (The existing atom-2 tests cover live-reattach-with-answer and dead-pane fresh-spawn fallback; do not weaken them.)

DOCS (docs/oz-launch.md):
7. Update the resume description (around lines 55-61 and the founder-halt bullet around lines 80-82) to state the corrected behavior: `cocoder oz resume <runId>` continues a held run IN ITS EXISTING LIVE PANES (re-attaches; it never forks a second orchestrator), and respawns a fresh set only if those panes have died. Note that for a mid-run `ask-founder-continue` DECISION the preferred path is `founder-answer <runId> <answer>` (Oz chat) or `cocoder oz founder-answer <runId> <answer>` (CLI), which records the answer and resumes in place; remove any implication that resume spawns a fresh duplicate or that writing into the pane is the only/forbidden option that is now stale.

ACCEPTANCE: manual resume with live panes is no longer 409-refused (proceeds to reattach); founder-halt resume-in-place works; the unused guard/helper are gone (no deletable dead surface left); docs match behavior. Run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` (both green) and `tsc --noEmit` on both packages (exit 0). Report the commands, exit codes, and the renamed/added test names. Do not run git; the runner commits on verify pass.
